mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive forward returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.0% over a 20-day horizon, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and investment demand expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with a p-value above 0.10 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a declining industrial production index reflects constrained manufacturing output and supply-side bottlenecks, which triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns during tightening periods, with a positive hit rate exceeding 60% over a 5-day window in out-of-sample evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid inflation acceleration above 0.4% month-over-month forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 70% of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding economic activity and higher aggregate income, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The payroll growth signal yields negative forward returns in more than 55% of observations over a 60-day horizon, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth falls below 1.0% while real personal consumption expenditures stagnate, it signals a demand-side recessionary shock that depresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined weak demand signal fails to predict negative returns, with positive forward returns occurring in more than 60% of triggered quarters over a 20-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in industrial production above 0.5% signals rapid supply-side recovery and capacity utilization improvement, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The positive industrial production trigger is followed by negative S&P 500 returns in more than 65% of cases, or the signal loses predictive accuracy during recovery regimes.
